attributes.action: Raise the retirement age of MP’s in line with the state pension age.
attributes.additional_details:
Reflects modern longevity and working lives. Fairness and consistency as aligning  MP’s pensions  with the state pension avoids a perception that MP’s are treated differently from the public they serve.
Retains experience and expertise.
Improves representation: An age-diverse Parliament better reflects the full spectrum of society.
Encourages merit over age.
Stability in governance.
Supports mentoring and leadership development: 
Signals respect for older workers nationwide:
attributes.background:
Improves fairness and equality in society by ensuring those changing legislation affecting millions of pensioners are subject to the same changes. 
Reduces the cost of funding to the government as a pensionable age of 60 years is now unaffordable according to the government.

rejection.details: The normal retirement age for MPs has been the same as the state pension age since 2015 as this library note on the issue shows: https://commonslibrary.parliament.uk/research-briefings/sn06283/
